page: Amazon Web Services g6e.48xlarge is a graphics-intensive, high-density server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. The hardware configuration features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 192 dedicated vCPUs (96 physical cores) operating at 3.4 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 system memory. Acceleration is driven by eight NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 358 GB of VRAM. Local storage is bundled with 7600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 400 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this server in the top tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and specialized compute tasks. The platform achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU metrics, and physics simulations. Memory performance is highly efficient, registering top-tier read-only bandwidth for small cached blocks and strong performance for larger uncached blocks. Additionally, the server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in ray tracing and LLM inference processing speeds across small, medium, and large models.

The integration of massive local NVMe SSD storage and high-speed memory architecture supports strong database operations, as evidenced by top-tier Redis SET throughput. This hardware profile offers high resource density, combining substantial compute, memory, and GPU resources into a single instance. The server is designed for highly demanding workloads, including large-scale machine learning inference, ray tracing, graphics rendering, and high-throughput database applications.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.48xlarge is a high-density, graphics-intensive server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server is equipped with eight NVIDIA L40S GPUs providing 358 GB of total VRAM. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional ray tracing capabilities, and top-tier LLM inference speeds across small, medium, and large models. It also delivers top-tier Redis SET operations and strong database performance. With a baseline network bandwidth of 400 Gbps, this server is optimized for demanding workloads such as large-scale LLM inference, graphics rendering, and high-throughput database applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.48xlarge is a graphics-intensive server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD, and 8 NVIDIA L40S GPUs, delivering top-tier LLM inference and ray tracing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD, and 8 NVIDIA L40S GPUs for top-tier LLM inference and graphics.
tagline: A high-density accelerator platform featuring eight GPUs, massive system memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 358 GB total VRAM for accelerated graphics and AI.
192 dedicated vCPUs via AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz.
1536 GB of DDR4 system memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models.
400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfers.
Optimized for large-scale machine learning, ray tracing, and database operations.
